successCriteria.description: src/modules/leave/leave.controller.ts exists and exports LeaveController class with methods: applyLeave, approveLeave, rejectLeave, cancelLeave, getLeaveRequest, getLeaveRequests, getLeaveBalance.
src/modules/leave/leave.routes.ts exists and exports registerLeaveRoutes(fastify, leaveController) that registers all leave endpoints with RBAC preHandlers and schema validation.

src/modules/leave/leave.schemas.ts exists and exports JSON Schema objects for each endpoint's request validation (applyLeaveSchema, approveLeaveSchema, rejectLeaveSchema, cancelLeaveSchema, getLeaveRequestSchema, getLeaveRequestsSchema, getLeaveBalanceSchema).
src/shared/types/auth.ts exists and exports AuthenticatedUser and AuthenticatedRequest interfaces.
src/shared/di/container.ts exists and exports setupContainer(fastify) that instantiates all repositories, services, and LeaveController, then registers leave routes.

All leave endpoints enforce RBAC: employees can only apply/cancel their own requests; managers can approve/reject requests for their subordinates; admins have full access. RBAC is enforced via Fastify preHandler hooks using authenticate and authorize decorators.
Input validation rejects invalid payloads with 400 status using Fastify schema validation defined in leave.schemas.ts.
State-changing operations (apply, approve, reject, cancel) result in audit log entries written by LeaveService (no additional audit logic in controller).
Jest unit tests for LeaveController and routes exist in tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.controller.test.ts and tests/unit/modules/leave/leave.routes.test.ts.

Integration tests in tests/integration/leave/leave.api.test.ts validate end-to-end flows using Fastify's inject method, covering apply, approve, reject, cancel, and balance retrieval with proper authentication and authorization.

constraints: All database access goes through repository interfaces (LeaveRepository, BalanceRepository, EmployeeRepository).
All state-changing operations (apply, approve, reject, cancel) must write an audit record via LeaveService.
Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing using Fastify schema validation.
No sensitive data (passwords, tokens, PII, financial data) in logs.
RBAC enforcement: All API endpoints enforce role-based access control via preHandler hooks (authenticate, authorize).
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections; all async errors are caught and handled.
Dependency direction: leave module depends on employee, policy, balance, notification; no reverse dependencies.
outOfScope: Implementing LeaveService, BalanceService, EmployeeService, or any repository logic (already completed in prior phases).
Modifying existing service or repository files.
Setting up the Fastify server instance or database connection (assumed to be provided).
Implementing the authenticate and authorize decorators on the Fastify instance (assumed to exist).
